title: podman-remote run alpine true -> exit status 126 on f29
body:
podman system tests reliably [failing on f29 only](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6010797106397184) with:
```
# podman-remote run quay.io/libpod/alpine_labels:latest true
[ rc=126 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
Very likely related to #3934, in which @rhatdan saw similar behavior in CI.

comments.body: This does not happen on f30 and f31?

f30: not that I've seen. 

f31: I don't think we have CI for it.

So far it's only failing in f29 and only in CI.

Giving up for today (and likely the week since I'll be traveling). Some good news: this is too obscure to be a showstopper.

First: this is not reproducible without the entire system test scaffolding: it only fails under BATS, and specifically only when the `parse_table` helper is used. I don't understand why, and have spent far too long on it already. 

There is one more clue:
```
# ./bin/podman-remote run alpine_labels /no/such/command
# echo $?
127
```
Note that the exit code is correct, but _there is no output_. No error message. The error message appears in the varlink window instead:
```
ERRO[4620] time="2019-09-16T23:46:17Z" level=error msg="container_linux.go:346: starting container process
caused \"exec: \\\"/no/such/command\\\": stat /no/such/command: no such file or directory\"\n"
container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused "exec: \"/no/such/command\": stat /no/such/command: no such file or direct
ory": OCI runtime command not found error
```
(This has me wondering how the test ever passed on f30 or anything else; but I can't look into that now).

Given all that, the only option I see is to re-disable the `030-run.bats` test when running remote:
```diff
 load helpers
 
 @test "podman run - basic tests" {
+    skip_if_remote "this test is currently broken on podman-remote"
     rand=$(random_string 30)
```
Could someone please make it so? Once again, I will be on PTO this week, traveling all of Tuesday and with very limited net access until next Monday.

The funny thing is that this is occasionally passing, which is how it got in, in the first place.  I have opened a PR to lessen the acquirements to return this error.
https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4052

We will see if this helps.
@edsantiago, are we still seeing the hiccup?

The original error (podman run alpine true -> 126) no longer reproduces on f29 with podman-1.6.0-2.fc29 but the second error is now worse:
```
# podman-remote run alpine /sdfsdf;echo $?
0
```
Actually it looks like we're back to the days where podman-remote ignores all exit codes:
```
# podman-remote run alpine false;echo $?
0
```
I wonder why we're not seeing those issues in the CI :thinking:

My mistake: I had dnf-installed podman-remote (1.5) instead of downloading the 1.6 build.

With 1.6.0 I get the correct exit status but incorrect (missing) message.

>I wonder why we're not seeing those issues in the CI

Because podman-remote is still slurping up stdin. I am going to submit a PR that fixes the problem, and will as a side-effect break CI for podman-remote (because podman-remote is broken).
I believe this is fixed in 1.6 but a different issue (#4096) prevents automated testing. Closing.
